text.title: Product Launch Planning
text.summary: Launch types, an 8-week countdown timeline, four required components, and a launch day sequence for coordinating a product introduction.

rule.statement: Choose launch type based on risk and audience size: soft launch (limited audience, low risk) → beta launch (early adopters, medium risk) → full launch (public, high risk). Do not skip stages for high-risk products.
rule.rationale: Full public launches are hard to walk back. A soft or beta launch lets you fix critical issues before they affect reputation at scale.

rule.statement: Start planning 6–8 weeks before launch day. T-8: define strategy. T-6: create assets. T-4: build pre-launch audience. T-2: finalize coordination. T-1: final checks. T-Day: execute. T+1: monitor and respond. T+2: retrospective.
rule.rationale: Launches planned in the final week lack pre-launch audience, have untested assets, and produce no momentum. The 8-week timeline is the minimum for a coordinated launch with measurable impact.

rule.statement: Positioning and messaging must be locked before asset creation. Key message, value proposition, target audience, and differentiation all need to be agreed before writing copy.
rule.rationale: Assets built before messaging alignment diverge and require rework. Messaging is the foundation; assets are the expression.

rule.statement: Minimum viable launch assets: landing page, demo or screenshots, announcement email, social posts, basic documentation. All must be ready at T-2.
rule.rationale: Missing assets on launch day create a gap between awareness (the announcement) and conversion (the landing page). Even one missing asset breaks the funnel.

rule.statement: Build a pre-launch audience before launch day: waitlist, email subscribers, social following, community relationships. The audience determines the floor for Day 1 metrics.
rule.rationale: A launch to zero audience produces zero traction regardless of product quality. Pre-launch audience building is the highest-leverage activity in the 8-week window.

rule.statement: Coordinate internally before going public: support team briefed, on-call engineer named, monitoring dashboards ready, social media accounts active and monitored.
rule.rationale: Launch day without internal coordination produces delayed responses to issues and confusion about who owns what. Each role needs to know its responsibilities before T-Day.

rule.statement: Launch on Tuesday or Wednesday, not Friday. For Product Hunt, launch at 12:01 AM Pacific Time to maximize voting window.
rule.rationale: Friday launches run into the weekend news cycle. Product Hunt voting resets at midnight PT; launching at 12:01 AM gives the maximum 24-hour window for votes.
